title: Lannea cotoneaster
taxonomy: Lannea cotoneaster is classified in the family Anacardiaceae, which includes other plants like the cashew and poison ivy. The genus Lannea is native to tropical and subtropical Africa, with several species found throughout the continent. Lannea cotoneaster, specifically, is a species of shrub or small tree that is native to southern Africa. It belongs to the order Sapindales, which includes other plants like the soapberry and the horse chestnut. The classification of Lannea cotoneaster reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Anacardiaceae family.

The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating various health issues.
